userStories.title: Add `nax prompts --init` command to export default templates
userStories.description:
Add a `--init` flag to the existing `nax prompts` CLI command. When invoked, it:

1. Creates `nax/templates/` directory in the project workdir
2. Writes the 4 default role-body templates as markdown files:
   - `nax/templates/test-writer.md`
   - `nax/templates/implementer.md`
   - `nax/templates/verifier.md`
   - `nax/templates/single-session.md`
3. Each file contains the output of `buildRoleTaskSection(role)` for the default variant (standard for implementer). Note: overrides only replace the role body — isolation, story context, and conventions are non-overridable.
4. Prints a summary of files written and instructions on how to activate overrides

If `nax/templates/` already has files, print a warning and do NOT overwrite unless `--force` is also passed. This prevents accidental loss of user customizations.

Each template should include a header comment explaining what the file controls and which sections are non-overridable (isolation, story context, conventions).

Implementation: extend `src/cli/prompts.ts` (existing command handler).

Run: NAX_SKIP_PRECHECK=1 bun test test/unit/cli/ test/integration/cli/ --timeout=60000
Commit: 'feat: add nax prompts --init to export default templates'
acceptanceCriteria: Writes 4 files: test-writer.md, implementer.md, verifier.md, single-session.md
Each file contains the default role-body content from buildRoleTaskSection()
Each file has a header comment explaining what it controls and what cannot be overridden
If files already exist, prints warning and exits without overwriting (no data loss)
Prints summary of files written and activation instructions after success
All existing prompts tests still pass

userStories.title: Auto-configure prompts.overrides when templates exist
userStories.description:
After `nax prompts --init` writes the template files, the user still needs to manually add `prompts.overrides` paths to `nax.config.json`. This story adds automatic wiring.

When `nax prompts --init` completes successfully:
1. Check if `nax.config.json` exists in workdir
2. If it exists and `prompts.overrides` is not already set, add the override paths:
   ```json
   {
     "prompts": {
       "overrides": {
         "test-writer": "nax/templates/test-writer.md",
         "implementer": "nax/templates/implementer.md",
         "verifier": "nax/templates/verifier.md",
         "single-session": "nax/templates/single-session.md"
       }
     }
   }
   ```
3. If `prompts.overrides` already has entries, do NOT modify — print a note that overrides are already configured
4. In headless/non-TTY mode, auto-write the config without prompting

Note: The override loader (`src/prompts/loader.ts`) already reads from these paths — no changes needed there.

Run: NAX_SKIP_PRECHECK=1 bun test test/unit/cli/ test/integration/cli/ test/integration/config/ --timeout=60000
Commit: 'feat: auto-configure prompts.overrides on init'
acceptanceCriteria: After writing templates, auto-adds prompts.overrides to nax.config.json if not already set
Does not overwrite existing prompts.overrides entries
Handles missing nax.config.json gracefully (prints manual instructions instead)
Config file is written with proper JSON formatting (2-space indent)
All existing config and prompts tests still pass
